introSection: Nightmare at Crack Axle Canyon was an enclosed steel roller coaster that operated at four different amusement parks in the United States.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The attraction first opened at Beech Bend in 1972 or earlier, and was called Jet Star.

In February 1985, it was purchased by the manager of Noble Park Funland for $118,000, and opened later that year.

The attraction later operated at Kentucky Kingdom from the opening of the park in 1987. It was known as Starchaser and was enclosed for the first time. It was sold at auction for $40,000 USD on April 15, 1989.

On June 7, 1996, the attraction opened at Darien Lake as Nightmare at Phantom Cave, representing an investment of $2 million.

In 1998, The Great Escape & Splashwater Kingdom announced that the attraction would open there in 1999 following an investment of $1.9 million. During construction, its name was changed to Nightmare at Crack Axle Canyon.

2006 was Nightmare's last season. Without any prior announcement, the ride was found to be closed for all of the 2007 season and all future seasons. In July 2007, the park stated that Nightmare at Crack Axle Canyon had been closed due to its low capacity. The building still stands and is visible from rides such as Canyon Blaster.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Single car trains. Riders are arranged inline in 4 rows for a total of 4 riders per car.

sections.title: Incidents
sections.content:
On August 30, 1975, a 17-year-old boy was killed while working on the Jet Star. He was removing blocks underneath the coaster with three other employees when a car began moving and struck his head.

In 1994, two cars collided resulting in multiple injuries.

fullText:
Nightmare at Crack Axle Canyon was an enclosed steel roller coaster that operated at four different amusement parks in the United States.

The attraction first opened at Beech Bend in 1972 or earlier, and was called Jet Star.

In February 1985, it was purchased by the manager of Noble Park Funland for $118,000, and opened later that year.

The attraction later operated at Kentucky Kingdom from the opening of the park in 1987. It was known as Starchaser and was enclosed for the first time. It was sold at auction for $40,000 USD on April 15, 1989.

On June 7, 1996, the attraction opened at Darien Lake as Nightmare at Phantom Cave, representing an investment of $2 million.

In 1998, The Great Escape & Splashwater Kingdom announced that the attraction would open there in 1999 following an investment of $1.9 million. During construction, its name was changed to Nightmare at Crack Axle Canyon.

2006 was Nightmare's last season. Without any prior announcement, the ride was found to be closed for all of the 2007 season and all future seasons. In July 2007, the park stated that Nightmare at Crack Axle Canyon had been closed due to its low capacity. The building still stands and is visible from rides such as Canyon Blaster.



Single car trains. Riders are arranged inline in 4 rows for a total of 4 riders per car.

On August 30, 1975, a 17-year-old boy was killed while working on the Jet Star. He was removing blocks underneath the coaster with three other employees when a car began moving and struck his head.

In 1994, two cars collided resulting in multiple injuries.
